questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:A particle of charge $q$ and mass $m$ is clamped rigidly at the circumference of a ring with mass $m$ and radius $R$. Initially ring is in vertical plane resting on a sufficiently rough horizontal surface with charge q at the same horizontal level as that of the centre of the ring. There exists uniform horizontal electric fields as shown. At $\mathrm{t}=0$ the system is let free. (Given that $\mathrm{qE}=\mathrm{mg}, \pi=\frac{22}{7}$ )Question:Work done by the electric field when the ring has rotated through $90^{\circ}$ is
questions.solution: Work done by electric field can be calculated by calculating. Displacement of charge in the direction of field which is (see figure) $\mathrm{S}=\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right) \mathrm{R}$ So work done $=\mathrm{qER}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right)=\frac{4}{7} \mathrm{mgR}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:A smooth circular wire track of radius $R$ and mass $m$ is lying in gravity free space. Two small conduting rings each of charge $+q$ and mass $m$ which can slide freely on circular track are released from rest when shortest distance between them is ' $R$ ' as shown in figure. At the instant when the two rings come to diametrically opposite pointsQuestion:Velocity of wire track is
questions.solution: From conservation of momentum along y-direction $0=\mathrm{mV}-2 \mathrm{mv} \quad \Rightarrow \mathrm{~V}=2 \mathrm{v}$ Now using energy conservation $\begin{aligned} & \frac{q^2}{4 \pi \epsilon_0 R}=\frac{1}{2} m \times 4 v^2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2 \times 2+\frac{\mathrm{q}^2}{4 \pi \epsilon_0(2 \mathrm{R})} \quad \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{q}^2}{24 \pi \epsilon_0 \mathrm{mR}}} \\ & \therefore \quad \mathrm{~V}=2 \mathrm{v} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph:A uniform disc of mass $m$ and radius R is made up of two halves, one half has charge +Q uniformly distributed over it \& another half has charge -Q uniformly distributed over it. This system is kept on rough surface in a uniform horizonal field E in a way so that line of seperation become parallel to field, system is released from rest, at $\mathrm{t}=0$ as shown in figure.Question:Torque of the electrostatic force about centre of mass at $t=0$ is-
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:A particle of charge $q$ and mass $m$ is clamped rigidly at the circumference of a ring with mass $m$ and radius $R$. Initially ring is in vertical plane resting on a sufficiently rough horizontal surface with charge q at the same horizontal level as that of the centre of the ring. There exists uniform horizontal electric fields as shown. At $\mathrm{t}=0$ the system is let free. (Given that $\mathrm{qE}=\mathrm{mg}, \pi=\frac{22}{7}$ )Question:Speed of the centre of mass of the system after $90^{\circ}$ rotation is
questions.solution: After $90^{\circ}$ rotation. Using work energy theorem $\frac{4}{7} \mathrm{mgR}+\mathrm{mgR}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{A}} \omega^2 ; \sqrt{\frac{11}{7} \frac{\mathrm{~g}}{\mathrm{R}}}=\omega ; \mathrm{v}_{\text {com }}=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{2} \omega=\sqrt{\frac{11}{7} \frac{\mathrm{~g}}{\mathrm{R}} \frac{\mathrm{R}^2}{4}}=\sqrt{\frac{11}{28} \mathrm{Rg}} .$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:A uniform disc of mass $m$ and radius R is made up of two halves, one half has charge +Q uniformly distributed over it \& another half has charge -Q uniformly distributed over it. This system is kept on rough surface in a uniform horizonal field E in a way so that line of seperation become parallel to field, system is released from rest, at $\mathrm{t}=0$ as shown in figure.Question:Acceleration of centre of the disc at $t=0$, if friction is sufficient to prevent slippling, is-
questions.solution:
$\tau_{\text {net }}=\mathrm{I} \alpha ; \tau_{\text {electric }}-\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{R}=\mathrm{I} \alpha$

From the equation of translatory motion $f_r=m a \Rightarrow \frac{8 Q R E}{3 \pi}-m a R=\frac{\mathrm{mR}^2}{2} \frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{R}}$
By solving above equation $\mathrm{a}=\frac{16 \mathrm{QE}}{9 \pi \mathrm{~m}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Three concentric spherical conductors A, B and C of radii R, $2 R$ and $4 R$ respectively. A and $C$ is shorted and $B$ is uniformly charged (charge $+Q$ )
Question: Charge on conductor A is
questions.solution: $$ \begin{aligned} & q_1+q_2=0 \\ & v_A=\frac{k q_1}{R}+\frac{k Q}{2 R}+\frac{k_2}{4 R} \\ & v_C=\frac{k q_1}{4 R}+\frac{k Q}{4 R}+\frac{k_2}{4 R} \\ & v_A=v_C \\ & \Rightarrow q_1=-Q / 3 \text { and } q_2=Q / 3 \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A small charged bead (mass = m, charge = Q) can slide on a circular frictionless, fixed insulating wire frame. A point like dipole (dipole moment P) is fixed at the centre of circle. Bead is released from rest from given position 'A'. (Neglect gravity)
Question: Choose incorrect statements.
options: Speed of bead is maximum at point ' $B$ '.
Normal force on the bead at point ' $A$ ' is zero
Tangential acceleration of bead at point ' $A$ ' is zero
questions.solution: Tangential acceleration of bead at point ' $A$ ' is zero
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: The electric field intensity at all points in space is given by $\vec{E}=\sqrt{3} \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}$ volts/metre. A square frame LMNO of side 1 metre is shown in figure. The point N lies in x - y plane. The initial angle between line ON and x -axis is $\theta=60^{\circ}$
Question: The magnitude of electric flux through area enclosed in square frame LMNO is -
questions.solution:
The direction of electric field is in $x-y$ plane as shown in figure
The magnitude of electric field is $E=\sqrt{E_x^2+E_y^2}=\sqrt{3+1}=2 V / m$ The direction of electric field is given by $\theta=\tan ^{-1} \frac{E_y}{E_x}=\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}=30^{\circ}$ Hence electric field is normal to square frame LMNO as shown in figure. $\therefore \text { electric flux }=\vec{E} \cdot \vec{A}=E A \cos 0=2 \times 1=2 \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m}$ $\therefore \mathrm{C}$ is correct option of Q. 15. Since flux is maximum at $\theta=60^{\circ}$, rotation by $30^{\circ}$ either way would lead to decrease in flux. $\therefore \mathrm{D}$ is correct option of Q .16 . Lines ON and NM are both normal to uniform electric field $\vec{E}$. Hence work done by electric field as a point charge $1 \mu \mathrm{C}$ is taken from O to M is zero.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
